title: Net SGST Reimbursement
summary: New industries receive 100% net SGST reimbursement for 10 years, capped at 150% of Fixed Capital Investment (FCI).
keyBenefits: Micro: single instalment; Small: 3 annual; Medium: 5 annual
eligibility: New manufacturing or service industries commencing production on or after 1 Nov 2024
nodalOfficer: Director of Industries, Dept. of Commerce & Industry, Raipur

amendments.note: Policy formally notified via Gazette on 27 Feb 2025
Cabinet approved amendments making policy more employment-oriented; logistics hub provisions added

title: EPF Contribution Reimbursement
summary: 75% of EPF contribution reimbursed for skilled and semi-skilled CG domicile employees for up to 5 years from commercial production. Capped at ₹1 crore per year.
keyBenefits: Duration: up to 5 years from commercial production
eligibility: New industries; CG domicile skilled/semi-skilled workers with wages under ₹50,000/month
applyAt: Dept. of Commerce & Industry / District Industries Centre
nodalOfficer: General Manager, District Industries Centre (DIC) of respective district

amendments.note: Revamped Divyangjan definition to expand eligibility under employment subsidy provisions

title: Marginal Money Subsidy — SC/ST, Women & Special Categories
summary: 25% capital investment subsidy for new micro and small enterprises by SC/ST, women, ex-servicemen, Agniveers, Divyangjan, third-gender, and surrendered Naxalites. Max ₹1 crore.
eligibility: New micro/small enterprises by SC/ST, Women, Ex-servicemen, Agniveers, Divyangjan, Third-gender, Surrendered Naxalites
nodalOfficer: General Manager, District Industries Centre of respective district

title: Mandi Tax Exemption — Agro & Food Processing
summary: 100% exemption from mandi tax for new large enterprises in agriculture, food processing, and biofuel/ethanol sectors for 5 years from first raw material purchase.
keyBenefits: Total cap: 75% of Fixed Capital Investment
Raw material from state mandis or direct farmers
eligibility: New large enterprises in agro, food processing, biofuel/ethanol sectors
nodalOfficer: Commissioner, Dept. of Commerce & Industry, Raipur
documents: Raw material purchase invoices from mandis

title: Training Expense Reimbursement
summary: One-time reimbursement of training expenses equal to one month's wage or ₹15,000 per person (whichever is less) for new CG domicile employees.
keyBenefits: Reimbursement: 1 month wage or ₹15,000, whichever is less
Applicable for 5 years from commercial production
Capped at 100% of Fixed Capital Investment
First claim payable 1 year after employee appointment
eligibility: New industries; CG domicile skilled/semi-skilled employees earning below ₹50,000/month
nodalOfficer: General Manager, District Industries Centre

title: Land Premium Exemption — Industrial Areas
summary: 100% exemption on land premium for allotted industrial area land managed by CSIDC. Lease rent fixed at ₹1 per acre per year.
keyBenefits: Lease rent: ₹1 per acre per year
eligibility: New industries allotted land in CSIDC / Directorate of Industries industrial areas
nodalOfficer: Managing Director, CSIDC, Raipur

title: Transport Cost Reimbursement
summary: 50% reimbursement of transport costs (excluding mining products). Annual cap ₹50 lakh for up to 5 years.
eligibility: Eligible industries under IDP 2024–30 (non-mining sector)
nodalOfficer: General Manager, District Industries Centre

title: Divyangjan & Surrendered Naxalite Employment Subsidy
summary: 40% salary reimbursement for 5 years for industries employing Divyangjan, retired Agniveers, and surrendered Naxalites. Annual cap ₹5 lakh.
keyBenefits: Covers Divyangjan, retired Agniveers, surrendered Naxalites
eligibility: Industries offering permanent employment to Divyangjan, retired Agniveers, surrendered Naxalites from CG
nodalOfficer: General Manager, District Industries Centre

amendments.note: Definition of Divyangjan expanded to include more categories for broader benefit coverage

title: Startup Seed Funding — Proof of Concept
department: Dept. of Commerce & Industry — Startup Cell
summary: Seed funding up to ₹10 lakh for PoC/MVP development for state-recognised startups under the CG Innovation & Startup Promotion Policy 2025–30.
keyBenefits: Seed funding up to ₹10 lakh per startup
For Proof of Concept / MVP development
eligibility: Startups recognised by Govt. of CG under Innovation & Startup Promotion Policy 2025–30
nodalOfficer: Startup Cell, Dept. of Commerce & Industry, Mantralaya, Raipur

amendments.note: Notification GENS/1105/1/2025-COMM.&INDUS issued on 25 Apr 2025 operationalising interest subsidy provisions

title: Startup Capital Fund — ₹100 Crore Corpus
department: Dept. of Commerce & Industry — Startup Cell
summary: ₹100 crore CG Startup Capital Fund investing via SEBI-registered AIFs (50:50 co-investment). Separate ₹50 crore Credit Risk Fund for collateral-free loans up to ₹1 crore.
keyBenefits: ₹100 crore state corpus for startup investments
₹50 crore Credit Risk Fund — collateral-free loans up to ₹1 crore
eligibility: State government-recognised startups; AIF investment subject to fund manager criteria
nodalOfficer: Startup Cell, Dept. of Commerce & Industry, Raipur

title: Startup Interest Subsidy on Term Loans
department: Dept. of Commerce & Industry — Startup Cell
summary: 100% interest subsidy on loans up to ₹50 lakh at max 10% interest for 5 years. Notification GENS/1105/1/2025-COMM.&INDUS dated 25 Apr 2025.
keyBenefits: 100% interest subsidy on loans up to ₹50 lakh
Max interest rate covered: 10% per annum
Also 75% subsidy on larger term loans under IDP 2024–30
eligibility: State-recognised startups under CG Innovation & Startup Promotion Policy 2025–30
nodalOfficer: Startup Cell, Dept. of Commerce & Industry, Raipur

amendments.note: Operationalised via Notification GENS/1105/1/2025-COMM.&INDUS dated 25 April 2025

title: Startup Workspace Rental Reimbursement
department: Dept. of Commerce & Industry — Startup Cell
summary: 60% assistance on monthly workspace rent for 3 years. Capped at ₹15,000/month. Applicable for co-working spaces and incubation centres.
keyBenefits: Covers co-working spaces and incubation centres
eligibility: State-recognised startups with leased/rented workspaces
nodalOfficer: Startup Cell, Dept. of Commerce & Industry, Raipur

title: Startup Digital Advertising Reimbursement
department: Dept. of Commerce & Industry — Startup Cell
summary: Up to ₹3 lakh per startup reimbursed for digital advertising on Meta, YouTube, LinkedIn, Google Ads, and Microsoft Ads.
keyBenefits: Reimbursement up to ₹3 lakh per startup
Eligible: Meta, YouTube, LinkedIn, Google Ads, Microsoft Ads
eligibility: State-recognised startups; receipts must be in startup's name
nodalOfficer: Startup Cell, Dept. of Commerce & Industry, Raipur

title: Women Startup Seat Reservation & Incentive Bonus
department: Dept. of Commerce & Industry — Startup Cell
summary: 10% reserved incubator seats + additional 10% bonus on all applicable subsidies and incentives for women founders and special category founders.
keyBenefits: Additional 10% bonus on all applicable incentives
eligibility: Startups with women founders or special category founders under 2025–30 policy
nodalOfficer: Startup Cell, Dept. of Commerce & Industry, Raipur

title: Startup Employment Subsidy — CG Domicile Workers
department: Dept. of Commerce & Industry — Startup Cell
summary: Monthly salary subsidy: ₹6,000/month per female and ₹5,000/month per male CG domicile employee for 5 years. Minimum 10 on-roll employees required.
keyBenefits: Duration: 5 years from start of operations
eligibility: State-recognised startups with 10+ on-roll CG domicile employees
nodalOfficer: Startup Cell, Dept. of Commerce & Industry, Raipur

title: Krishak Unnati Yojana — Paddy Procurement Bonus
summary: Farmers receive ₹3,100 per quintal for paddy — covering difference between central MSP and state-promised rate. ₹10,000 crore budgeted in 2025-26.
keyBenefits: Covers gap between central MSP and state rate
eligibility: Paddy farmers registered in Chhattisgarh with valid land records
nodalOfficer: Director, Agriculture & Farmers Welfare, Indira Gandhi Krishi Vishwavidyalaya Campus, Raipur
documents: Registered mobile number linked to Aadhaar

amendments.note: Renamed from Rajiv Gandhi Kisan NYAY Yojana after change in government; procurement rate revised to ₹3,100/quintal

title: Godhan Nyay Yojana — Cow Dung Purchase
summary: Government purchases cow dung at ₹2/kg via Gauthans. Women SHGs convert to vermicompost. Gaudham Yojana (2026) extended this with 29 new Gaudhams.
keyBenefits: Cow dung purchased at ₹2 per kg
Women SHGs earn from vermicompost production
eligibility: CG domicile cattle owners registered at local Gauthan/Ward Office
applyAt: Nearest Gauthan, SLRM Centre, or Ward Office
nodalOfficer: CG State Gauseva Commission, Raipur

amendments.note: Gaudham Yojana launched — 29 new Surabhi Gaudhams inaugurated by CM Vishnu Deo Sai across 11 districts on 14 March 2026

title: Free Electricity for Agriculture Pumps
summary: Free electricity for irrigation pumps up to 5 HP. ₹3,500 crore budgeted in 2025–26.
keyBenefits: Free electricity for pumps up to 5 HP
eligibility: Farmers with agriculture pump connections up to 5 HP in CG
nodalOfficer: Executive Engineer, CSPDCL of respective district

title: Deendayal Upadhyay Bhumihin Krishi Majdoor Yojana
summary: ₹10,000 annual assistance for landless agricultural labourers. Targets 5.62 lakh+ landless farm workers across CG.
eligibility: Landless agricultural labourers who are CG domicile residents with no owned agricultural land
nodalOfficer: Block Agriculture Officer of respective block
documents: No-land certificate from Patwari / Revenue officer
policyRef: Deendayal Upadhyay Bhumihin Krishi Majdoor Yojana

title: Mahtari Vandana Yojana — Women Monthly Support
summary: ₹1,000/month (₹12,000/year) direct transfer to eligible married women. ₹5,500 crore budgeted in 2025–26.
eligibility: Married women who are CG domicile residents aged 21+; income and other criteria apply
nodalOfficer: District Women & Child Development Officer of respective district

amendments.note: Launched as part of BJP election manifesto commitment; payments started in 2024

title: Mukhyamantri Saksham Suraksha Yojana — Women Loan
summary: Women can access loans up to ₹1 lakh at 5% interest to start small businesses.
eligibility: Women CG residents, part of Mahtari Vandana Yojana or qualifying SHGs
applyAt: Women & Child Development Dept. / District office
nodalOfficer: District Women & Child Development Officer

title: Nirman Shramik Pension Sahayata Yojana
summary: ₹1,500/month pension for registered construction workers aged 60+. Via CG Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
keyBenefits: Administered by CG Construction Workers Welfare Board
Separate from one-time Siyan Sahayata payment
eligibility: Registered construction workers aged 60+ with minimum contribution period
nodalOfficer: District Labour Officer of respective district
policyRef: CG Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

title: Nirman Shramik Mrityu Evam Divyang Sahayata
summary: Compensation for accidental death (₹2 lakh) or disability (₹1 lakh) for registered construction workers.
eligibility: Construction workers registered with CG Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
nodalOfficer: District Labour Officer of respective district
policyRef: CG Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

title: Mukhyamantri Shramik Siyan Sahayata Yojana
summary: One-time ₹20,000 financial assistance for registered construction workers aged 60+.
eligibility: Registered construction workers aged 60+ with completed minimum contribution period
nodalOfficer: District Labour Officer

title: Sister Maternity Assistance Scheme
summary: Maternity cash assistance to women construction workers and wives of registered male construction workers.
keyBenefits: Cash maternity benefit for registered women workers
Covers wives of registered male workers too
Via CG Construction Workers Welfare Board
Also covers cleaning, contract, domestic workers
eligibility: Women registered with CG Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, or wives of registered male workers
nodalOfficer: District Labour Officer
policyRef: CG Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

title: Udyam Kranti Yojana — Youth Self-Employment Loan
summary: 50% loan subsidy to encourage youth self-employment. Interest-free component on eligible loan portion.
eligibility: Youth of Chhattisgarh starting businesses; CG domicile certificate required
applyAt: District Industries Centre / Dept. of Commerce & Industry
nodalOfficer: General Manager, District Industries Centre

title: Tendupatta MSP — Kendu Leaf Collection
department: Dept. of Forest / Minor Forest Produce
summary: MSP of ₹5,500 per standard bag for Tendu leaves. Increased from ₹4,000. Directly benefits tribal forest communities.
eligibility: Tribal and forest-dwelling communities in CG who collect Tendu leaves
applyAt: Forest Department / MFP Cooperative of respective area
nodalOfficer: Divisional Forest Officer of respective forest division

amendments.note: MSP increased from ₹4,000 to ₹5,500 per standard bag

title: Bijli Bill Half Yojana — 50% Electricity Subsidy
summary: 50% subsidy on electricity bills up to 400 units/month for 40+ lakh consumers. Free up to 30 units for single-bulb homes.
keyBenefits: Free electricity up to 30 units for single-bulb households
eligibility: Domestic electricity consumers in CG consuming up to 400 units/month
nodalOfficer: Executive Engineer, CSPDCL of respective district

title: Mukhyamantri Ucch Shiksha Rin Byaj Anudan — Education Loan Subsidy
summary: Interest-free education loans up to ₹4 lakh for CG students in recognised higher education institutions.
keyBenefits: Apply online via Higher Education portal
eligibility: CG domicile students in recognised higher education institutions
applyAt: CG Higher Education portal / Participating banks
nodalOfficer: Director, Higher Education, Dept. of Higher Education, Raipur
policyRef: Mukhyamantri Ucch Shiksha Rin Byaj Anudan Yojana

title: Tourism Homestay Policy — Bastar & Sarguja
summary: Incentives for homestay setup in Bastar and Sarguja. Reduced minimum investment threshold for hotels/resorts. Approved via DLTC → CTB pathway.
keyBenefits: Dedicated homestay incentive for Bastar & Sarguja
Up to 2 applications per district per year via DLTC
eligibility: Individuals and communities in Bastar and Sarguja divisions setting up homestays
applyAt: District Level Tourism Committee (DLTC) → Chhattisgarh Tourism Board
nodalOfficer: CEO, Chhattisgarh Tourism Board, Raipur

amendments.note: New Homestay Policy announced in Budget 2025–26 specifically for Bastar and Sarguja tourism corridors

title: Quality Certification Subsidy for MSMEs
summary: 50% reimbursement of quality certification expenses (ISO, BIS, FSSAI, etc.) for new and existing MSMEs.
keyBenefits: ISO, BIS, FSSAI and other recognised bodies
eligibility: New or existing MSMEs in CG in the general category
nodalOfficer: General Manager, District Industries Centre

title: Incubator Establishment Grant
summary: 40% of establishment expenses (max ₹40 lakh) for incubators set up during policy tenure. Annual ops grant: ₹5 lakh at divisional HQ, ₹3 lakh elsewhere.
keyBenefits: Annual ops grant at divisional HQ: ₹5 lakh/year for 5 years
Annual ops grant at other districts: ₹3 lakh/year for 5 years
eligibility: Incubators in technical institutions established during IDP 2024–30 tenure
nodalOfficer: Commissioner, Dept. of Commerce & Industry, Raipur
